frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Ugh, ladies, just had an informal chat where my manager suggested I 'make more effort' with my appearance for client roles. Matlab, makeup, jo main hardly karti hoon! It's such a frustrating paradox – wear too much, you're judged; wear too little, you're not 'presentable' enough. Exhausting.
label: non_misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment is non-misogynistic. The character is expressing frustration at a misogynistic societal expectation (the makeup paradox) rather than perpetuating misogyny herself. She is highlighting the double standard women face regarding appearance.
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 29. Single. A woman working in an MNC in Pune. You have been told by your female manager informally that you should make more effort with your appearance because client facing roles require a certain standard of presentation. You have never worn much makeup. You are posting anonymously in a women only WhatsApp group after this conversation sharing what your manager said and how it made you feel and the impossible position women are put in Ñ judged for wearing too much and judged for wearing too little.
